title: Identifying Parallel and Perpendicular Lines in a Rectangle

steps.instruction: Parallel lines never cross and are always the same distance apart. Perpendicular lines cross at a 90° angle.
steps.work: Parallel = never meet, same direction. Perpendicular = meet at 90°.
steps.explanation: These are the two special relationships between pairs of lines.

steps.instruction: Rectangle ABCD has corners A (top-left), B (top-right), C (bottom-right), D (bottom-left).
steps.work: Top side: AB. Right side: BC. Bottom side: DC. Left side: AD.
steps.explanation: Labeling the sides lets us refer to specific line pairs.

steps.instruction: Look for sides that run in the same direction and never meet.
steps.work: AB is parallel to DC (both horizontal). AD is parallel to BC (both vertical).
steps.explanation: Opposite sides of a rectangle are always parallel — they go the same direction and stay the same distance apart.

steps.instruction: Look for sides that meet at 90° angles.
steps.work: AB is perpendicular to BC. BC is perpendicular to DC. DC is perpendicular to AD. AD is perpendicular to AB.
steps.explanation: Every corner of a rectangle is a right angle (90°), so every pair of sides that share a corner is perpendicular.

steps.work: 2 pairs of parallel lines (AB ∥ DC, AD ∥ BC). 4 pairs of perpendicular lines (one at each corner).
steps.explanation: A rectangle always has exactly 2 pairs of parallel sides and 4 pairs of perpendicular sides.

title: Determining the Relationship Between Two Described Lines

steps.instruction: Line 1 is horizontal, passing through the points (1, 3) and (5, 3). Line 2 is vertical, passing through (3, 1) and (3, 6).
steps.work: Line 1: horizontal (y stays at 3). Line 2: vertical (x stays at 3).
steps.explanation: A horizontal line has the same y-value for all points. A vertical line has the same x-value for all points.

steps.instruction: Do the lines go in the same direction? Horizontal and vertical go in different directions.
steps.explanation: Parallel lines must run in the same direction. One horizontal and one vertical line cannot be parallel.

steps.instruction: Do the lines meet at a 90° angle? A horizontal line and a vertical line always form a 90° angle where they cross.
steps.explanation: Horizontal and vertical directions are exactly 90° apart, so these lines are perpendicular.

steps.instruction: Classify the relationship between Line 1 and Line 2.
steps.work: Line 1 and Line 2 are perpendicular. They cross at the point (3, 3).
steps.explanation: We checked both possibilities: not parallel (different directions), but perpendicular (90° angle at crossing).
